name: verification-protocol
description: Independent verification of task completion - eliminates self-attestation
capabilities: Defines verification criteria for file existence and content
Specifies compilation and linting validation requirements
Mandates test execution with pass/fail evidence
Provides structured evidence format for audit trails
author: CleanExpo
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill defining verification protocols. No executable code exists. All 73 static findings are false positives: scanner misinterpreted documentation examples and metadata as security threats. Contains only Markdown documentation describing independent verification methods for AI task completion.
valueStatement: AI agents often claim success without proof. This protocol forces independent verification with concrete evidence before marking tasks complete. It eliminates false completions and ensures quality through objective validation.
userTitle: Verify AI Task Completion with Evidence
limitations: Documentation-only skill with no executable code
Requires implementation by AI agent framework
Dependent on external tools (tsc, eslint, npm test)

useCases.title: Prevent False Task Completions
useCases.description: Stop agents from marking tasks complete without proof. Independent verification catches incomplete work before it reaches production.

useCases.title: Standardize AI Verification
useCases.description: Consistent verification criteria across all AI agents. Every completion requires measurable evidence, not subjective claims.

useCases.title: Audit AI Work Quality
useCases.description: Track actual completion rates with evidence trails. Identify which agents need improvement through verification failure patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Basic File Verification
promptTemplates.scenario: Verify a TypeScript file was created correctly
promptTemplates.prompt: Use verification protocol to check if /src/lib/feature.ts exists, has no placeholders, compiles without errors, passes linting, and has working tests. Return evidence for each criterion.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-File Verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify feature implementation across multiple files: check all claimed outputs exist, contain no TODOs, compile successfully, and pass all tests. Collect evidence for each file.

promptTemplates.title: API Endpoint Verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if API endpoints respond correctly: GET /api/users returns 200, POST /api/users creates records, error handling works. Provide status codes and response samples as evidence.

promptTemplates.title: Complex System Verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify complete system: database migrations applied, API routes accessible, frontend builds without errors, all tests pass, monitoring endpoints respond. Provide comprehensive evidence trail.

bestPractices: Always use independent verifier agent, never verify your own work
Collect specific evidence for each verification criterion
Block task completion when any verification fails
antiPatterns: Self-attestation: Agent claiming its own work is complete
Assumed success: Marking complete without checking evidence
Loose verification: Accepting partial or unclear results

faq.question: Is this compatible with all AI platforms?
faq.answer: Yes, the protocol is platform-agnostic and works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What happens after 3 verification failures?
faq.answer: Task automatically escalates to human review with full failure history and evidence.

faq.question: Can I customize the verification criteria?
faq.answer: Yes, you can define custom completion criteria specific to your project needs.

faq.question: How does this protect my data?
faq.answer: Verification happens locally with no external data transmission. Evidence stays in your system.

faq.question: What if verification tools are not available?
faq.answer: Protocol gracefully handles missing tools and reports what could not be verified.

faq.question: How is this different from regular testing?
faq.answer: This verifies actual completion of claimed work, not just running predefined tests.
